high_concern_flags.detail: Page cites Idaho Code § 72-1366 per task brief as the back-UI premium + penalty source. Recommend verification of the exact civil-penalty language against the current 2026 Idaho statute compilation before relying on a specific penalty figure for a client matter.

Page states Idaho workers' comp coverage is mandatory at 1+ employees, citing Idaho Code § 72-301 with statutory exclusions at § 72-212 (sole proprietors, certain agricultural and household workers). Treatment is consistent with Idaho Industrial Commission guidance but specific exclusions list should be verified against the current statute before any client-pricing decision.

Compliance cache for Idaho already records two prompt_correction flags (HB 40 not HB 521 for the 5.3% flat rate; 2026 SUI wage base $58,300 not $55,300). This worker-classification page uses the corrected figures (5.3% flat rate, $58,300 wage base) where they appear in the cost-of-misclassification table.

spec_notes: Modelled on Georgia sibling structure (most recent V2 common-law page) with Idaho-specific data swaps and Idaho Code citations substituted throughout.
Right-of-control test treatment quoted from Idaho Code § 72-1316 (SUI covered employment) and Idaho Code § 72-102(12) (workers' comp employee definition).
Workers' comp coverage threshold (1 employee) per Idaho Code § 72-301 with limited statutory exclusions at § 72-212.
Section 530 federal safe harbour treatment distinguished from California ABC (where no equivalent state safe harbour exists).
No Idaho ABC test, no profession-by-profession carve-out list, no gig-economy carve-out (unlike Georgia HB 389). Cleanest classification regime in the West.
Three named Idaho scenarios (Logan — Boise software dev; Sage — Coeur d'Alene freelance designer; Wyatt — Idaho Falls ops contractor) per task brief and V2 spec.
Capsule = 4-paragraph stake/size/ack/promise. Body prose statute-free per V2; statutes confined to Source columns of data tables.
End-of-page disclaimer uses task-brief exact specification.

stage_provenance.research: Used compliance cache idaho.json plus task-brief guidance for Idaho Code § 72-1316 (covered employment), Idaho Code § 72-102(12) (workers' comp employee), Idaho Code § 72-1366 (penalty), and Idaho Code § 63-3035 (state withholding). Cross-checked against Georgia sibling (most recent V2 common-law page) and California sibling (ABC contrast) already documented.
stage_provenance.brief: Sprint mode — generated directly in deploy directory without separate brief.md. Autopilot mode, no human approval pause.
stage_provenance.draft: Single-pass mirror of Georgia sibling structure with Idaho-specific content swapped throughout. V2 editorial standard applied at draft time.
stage_provenance.images: Hero reused from Idaho sibling (state-income page). Inline stamp-block visual composed in-HTML per CSS classes already in _shared/child-base.css. No gpt-image-1 calls.
